title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox start 2026 season with a 1-5 record, worst since 2019, after being swept by the Astros.
Red Sox pitching rotation struggles, allowing 11 earned runs over 12 innings, with a collective 5.22 ERA.
Team defense falters with six errors in six games, continuing issues from previous season's league-leading 116 errors.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs will face trial on May 4 in Dedham District Court for strangulation and assault charges.
The charges against Diggs involve an alleged incident with his personal chef over a financial dispute.
Diggs's lawyer claims the allegations are motivated by an unresolved employee-employer financial disagreement.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide linked to a 2023 fatal crash.
Investigation revealed a lack of notification to the Middlesex DA's office about the crash details by local and state police.
Suffolk County DA Kevin Hayden is prosecuting due to conflict of interest, as Quigley was tied to Middlesex DA's office.

title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alyssa Partsch, 33, sentenced to 15-20 years for fatally stabbing Jazreanna Sheppard in July 2023.
Partsch pleaded guilty to manslaughter in a plea deal that reduced her charge from second-degree murder.
The stabbing followed an online feud and occurred at the Park Street MBTA station in Boston.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Connecticut Sun WNBA team is relocating to Houston and will be rebranded as the Houston Comets.
Kevin Durant expressed his support for the move, jokingly noting that Connecticut fans have UConn women's basketball.
Boston's attempt to acquire the team was blocked, despite a higher bid than the accepted offer from Houston.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Carlos Narvaez was scratched from the Red Sox lineup against the Houston Astros hours before the game began.
Manager Alex Cora cited unspecified reasons for the change, replacing Narvaez with Connor Wong as the catcher.
Narvaez confirmed his removal was not due to injury and preferred keeping specifics private between him and Cora.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots plan to pick up Christian Gonzalez's fifth-year option, keeping him through the 2027 season.
Gonzalez will earn $18.1 million in 2027 if the option is picked up, a significant increase from his previous salary.
Gonzalez, a 2023 first-round pick, earned a Pro Bowl nod and was a second-team All-Pro selection in 2024.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm scores seven points in his last six games, boosting the Bruins' playoff ambitions.
Lindholm, now healthy, finds form on the third line with five goals and two assists recently.
The Bruins thrive with Pastrnak's 66 assists and second-line dominance from Zacha and Arvidsson.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson will run the 2026 Boston Marathon with Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation to support trauma care.
Davidson's motivation stems from the 2013 Marathon bombings and Boston's resilient response to the tragedy.
The Boston Marathon holds personal significance for Davidson, reflecting her long-term connection to the event and city.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston is facing a $48.4 million budget deficit this fiscal year due to snow removal and police overtime costs.
City officials are contemplating tapping into $1.2 billion in reserve funds to close the budget gap.
Boston Public Schools face a separate $53 million deficit, potentially leading to 400 job cuts next year.
